text: The surgeon uses a NovaGrasp device to retract the sigmoid colon towards the left lateral pelvic sidewall during a robotic ventral mesh rectopexy for internal rectal prolapse.
The surgeon incises the peritoneum lateral to the rectum and progressively lengthens the incision, exposing underlying structures.
The surgeon uses a monopolar curved scissor to perform dissection anterior to the rectum, developing the rectovaginal septum sharply between the rectum and the vagina.
The procedure is performed without mobilization of the tissue posterior to the rectum, maintaining the anatomical position of the posterior structures.
The surgeon trims a piece of Surgimand mesh to size and places it in the abdomen. The mesh is then sutured to the most distal part of the rectum using interrupted 2-0 Ticron sutures.
The surgeon uses additional two-oh ticron sutures to further secure the Surgimand mesh to the rectum, ensuring stable fixation.
The surgeon fixates the mesh to the sacral promontory using interrupted sutures, then tests the mesh to ensure it has appropriate tension.
The surgeon closes the peritoneum over the mesh using a continuous V-lock suture, ensuring the mesh is securely covered within the abdominal cavity.
